ec2_instance_port_ssh_exposed_to_interne.Finding: Instance i-099a6b67964e6b67f has SSH exposed to 0.0.0.0/0 on public IP address 100.26.21.77 in public subnet subnet-0363ff4534fffc519.

ec2_securitygroup_allow_ingress_from_int.Recommendation: Use a Zero Trust approach. Narrow ingress traffic as much as possible. Consider north-south as well as east-west traffic.

iam_role_administratoraccess_policy.Finding: IAM Role admin has AdministratorAccess policy attached.

iam_user_mfa_enabled_console_access.Finding: User cloud_user has Console Password enabled but MFA disabled.
iam_user_mfa_enabled_console_access.Recommendation: Enable MFA for the user's account. MFA is a simple best practice that adds an extra layer of protection on top of your user name and password. Recommended to use hardware keys over virtual MFA.

s3_account_level_public_access_blocks.Finding: Block Public Access is not configured for the account 994728658518.

ec2_instance_detailed_monitoring_enabled.Recommendation: Enable detailed monitoring for EC2 instances to gain better insights into performance metrics.

iam_check_saml_providers_sts.Recommendation: Enable SAML provider and use temporary credentials. You can use temporary security credentials to make programmatic requests for AWS resources using the AWS CLI or AWS API (using the AWS SDKs ). The temporary credentials provide the same permissions that you have with use long-term security credentials such as IAM user credentials. In case of not having SAML provider capabilities prevent usage of long-lived credentials.

iam_policy_attached_only_to_group_or_rol.Finding: User cloud_user has the policy Playground_AWS_Sandbox attached.
iam_policy_attached_only_to_group_or_rol.Recommendation: Remove any policy attached directly to the user. Use groups or roles instead.

cloudtrail_kms_encryption_enabled.Finding: Multiregion trail AWS-Cost has encryption disabled.
cloudtrail_kms_encryption_enabled.Recommendation: This approach has the following advantages: You can create and manage the CMK encryption keys yourself. You can use a single CMK to encrypt and decrypt log files for multiple accounts across all regions. You have control over who can use your key for encrypting and decrypting CloudTrail log files. You can assign permissions for the key to the users. You have enhanced security.

ec2_ebs_volume_encryption.Recommendation: Encrypt all EBS volumes and Enable Encryption by default You can configure your AWS account to enforce the encryption of the new EBS volumes and snapshot copies that you create. For example, Amazon EBS encrypts the EBS volumes created when you launch an instance and the snapshots that you copy from an unencrypted snapshot.

ec2_ebs_volume_snapshots_exists.Finding: Snapshots not found for the EBS volume vol-0c84363f85fc20dd7.
ec2_ebs_volume_snapshots_exists.Recommendation: Creating point-in-time EBS snapshots periodically will allow you to handle efficiently your data recovery process in the event of a failure, to save your data before shutting down an EC2 instance, to back up data for geographical expansion and to maintain your disaster recovery stack up to date.

s3_bucket_event_notifications_enabled.Finding: S3 Bucket cf-templates-1xw5869i06gah-us-east-1 does not have event notifications enabled.

s3_bucket_object_versioning.Finding: S3 Bucket cf-templates-1xw5869i06gah-us-east-1 has versioning disabled.

securityhub_enabled.Recommendation: Security Hub is Regional. When you enable or disable a security standard, it is enabled or disabled only in the current Region or in the Region that you specify.
